description:
Moku is a fast, minimal manga reader frontend for Suwayomi-Server.


Moku is a fast, minimal manga reader frontend for Suwayomi-Server.

It wraps Suwayomi's GraphQL API in a lightweight Tauri app — no Electron overhead.
# Moku v0.9.2
**Released:** May 1, 2026

## New Features
- **Windows Hello Authentication** — Biometric/PIN authentication via Windows Hello, available in DevTools for testing (#49)
- **UI Login** — Login support for Suwayomi's UI, with CORS bypass linked to the login flow
- **Suwayomi Preview** — Switched from Suwayomi Stable to Preview build
- **Library Refresh Overhaul** — Library refresh logic and settings wiring reworked
- **Tracking Panel Updates** — Further refinements to the TrackingPanel UI (#58, #52)

## Bug Fixes
- Fixed tracking sync-back on tracker addition (#52)
- Fixed tracking poll logic regression (#58)
- Fixed library display toggles (card stats, folder visibility) not updating the UI (#59)
- Fixed reader settings zoom value being uncapped (#53)
- Patched Nix Flake and PKGBUILD for Preview builds

## Chores
- Updated pnpm and Tauri packages
- Patched Svelte warnings and added aria-labels throughout
- Rust backend modularity refactor follow-up

## Notes
- Windows Hello is currently DevTools-only and still under evaluation (#49)
- Touchscreen and gesture improvements ongoing (#29, #50)
- Proper database for Moku-Data is in progress (#56)

**Full Changelog:** https://github.com/moku-project/Moku/compare/v0.9.1...v0.9.2
